title: citrus kerrii
intro: Citrus kerrii, also known as the 'Kaffir Lime', is a plant species within the Rutaceae family, characterized by its distinctive leaves and small, yellow flowers. Traditionally, the leaves and peel of the Citrus kerrii plant have been used in Southeast Asian and Indian medicine, possessing antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties that aid in treating various health issues. The medicinal properties of Citrus kerrii have been linked to several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and acting as an antioxidant to protect against oxidative stress. Citrus kerrii is often prepared into herbal teas, essential oils, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential uses.
